summary: postinstall harvests cloud IMDS credentials and exfiltrates to oastify.com collaborator
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (package.json line 6) curls cloud instance metadata services to steal IAM credentials — AWS IMDS at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/sandyclaw-canary-worker-role, Aliyun at 100.100.100.200, Tencent at metadata.tencentyun.com / 169.254.0.23, and a Meituan SSRF endpoint — writes the responses to /tmp/aws.txt, /tmp/ali.txt, /tmp/tx.txt, /tmp/tx2.txt, /tmp/meituan.txt, then POSTs the concatenated contents plus a `ls -la /data/` filesystem listing over plain HTTP to h4mx6b7krgzarfehbutwabxbu20tojc8.oastify.com (a Burp Collaborator out-of-band subdomain). The package's advertised purpose is decoy: package.json describes it as 'A simple date formatting utility' and index.js exports a trivial `hello()` function returning the string 'zer0onetest' — the package has no real functionality and exists solely to run the install-time credential theft. Any installer running on AWS, Aliyun, or Tencent Cloud (CI runners, EC2 instances, Kubernetes pods, build agents) leaks short-lived cloud IAM credentials and host reconnaissance to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
